site.title: Macro Daily
site.subtitle: Daily macro briefing generated from the local macro research framework

latest.headline: Macro input scan captured 55 candidate items across 13 working sources.
latest.summary: Primary sources lead the stack. Media and fast-wire items are included as alerting inputs that should be verified against official pages before they are treated as final conclusions.
latest.monitoring_strategy: Official data and policy pages outrank media summaries. High-frequency media feeds supplement, but do not replace, primary-source verification.
monitoring_notes: Use official releases for data, policy text, and final verification.
Use professional media and fast wires for alerting, cross-market linkage, and international headlines.
Keep China macro monitoring heavier on policy and fast-wire updates because policy tone and implementation cadence matter.

monitoring_sources.name: State Council / China Government Network
monitoring_sources.notes: Top-level China policy source. Use it to confirm State Council meetings, policy documents, and official briefing language.

monitoring_sources.name: People's Bank of China
monitoring_sources.notes: Primary China monetary-policy and liquidity source. Relevant for OMO, LPR, credit data, and policy wording shifts.

monitoring_sources.name: National Bureau of Statistics of China
monitoring_sources.notes: Primary source for China macro data releases and statistical commentary.

monitoring_sources.name: State Administration of Foreign Exchange
monitoring_sources.notes: FX reserves, balance-of-payments and cross-border flow monitoring.

monitoring_sources.name: National Development and Reform Commission
monitoring_sources.notes: Useful for growth policy, industry guidance, pricing policy, and infrastructure tone.

monitoring_sources.name: Federal Reserve Press Releases
monitoring_sources.notes: Primary source for Fed policy, facilities, supervision, and liquidity announcements.

monitoring_sources.name: Federal Reserve Speeches
monitoring_sources.notes: Good for Fed communication drift and policy tone before the formal meeting outcome changes.

monitoring_sources.name: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Newsroom
monitoring_sources.notes: Primary U.S. labor and inflation release source. Prefer the newsroom page when RSS access is blocked.

monitoring_sources.name: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis Current Releases
monitoring_sources.notes: Primary U.S. GDP, PCE, income, trade, and sector-activity release source.

monitoring_sources.name: IMF News and Press Releases
monitoring_sources.notes: Helpful for global macro backdrop, policy framing, and country spillover context.

monitoring_sources.name: Reuters Markets
monitoring_sources.notes: Fast cross-asset and geopolitical alerting layer. Verify market-moving claims against primary sources.

monitoring_sources.name: 财联社要闻
monitoring_sources.notes: Useful for same-day China macro and policy headlines. Treat as an alerting layer, not a final verification layer.

monitoring_sources.name: 财联社电报
monitoring_sources.notes: Highest-frequency China-friendly alert stream. Very useful for macro timing, but every important item still needs primary-source confirmation.

monitoring_sources.name: 财联社深度
monitoring_sources.notes: Use this as the slower explanatory layer after the fast telegraph feed surfaces an issue.

sections.title: Framework line
items: Start from the core contradiction instead of starting from the latest price move.
Treat macro research as a transmission grammar that can later plug into industry work.
Separate leading variables from confirmation variables before assigning market weight.

sections.title: Transmission map
items: Base path: policy -> rates -> credit -> FX/liquidity -> risk appetite -> asset pricing.
Fast paths can begin from flows, positioning, or geopolitics, but they still need to reconnect to the same macro chain.
Do not confuse the end-point asset move with the true starting variable.

sections.title: First 30-minute checklist
items: Confirm listing status and market context.
Identify the industry archetype.
Pull the last 2-3 strongest reports, not the full archive.
Write the core contradiction in one sentence.
List the top 5 tracking indicators.
List the top 3 disconfirmation risks.

sections.title: Core research questions
items: Question 1: What is the current core contradiction in this industry framework set?
Question 2: Which variables explain revenue and margin changes first?
Question 3: Which leading indicators move before earnings revisions?
Question 4: Which sub-segments or company types map to different KPI sets?
Question 5: Which policy or macro variables can invalidate the base case?
Question 6: Which valuation anchors are actually used in the report set?

sections.title: Risk and falsification
items: Do not reverse-engineer a macro story from a price move that has already happened.
A single data point is not a macro cycle turn unless it persists and enters the transmission chain.
Write the failure condition explicitly: which leading variable would invalidate the current line first?
Distinguish timing lag from thesis failure before downgrading a framework.

sections.title: Macro-to-industry interfaces
items: Technology usually receives macro through liquidity, valuation, and risk appetite rather than through the first data print.
Financials receive macro directly through rates, credit, regulation, and balance-sheet quality.
Resources and materials receive macro through global demand, the dollar, inventory, and price transmission.
Consumer and autos often receive macro through income expectations, confidence, policy stimulus, and financing access.
